phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your charm-orb pick threat beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — charm-orb pick threat edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your charm-orb pick threat beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — charm-orb pick threat edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight edge at the level-6 breakpoint: your charm-orb pick threat beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your charm-orb pick threat in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Slight deficit at two-plus items: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your charm-orb pick threat on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q both ways through the wave on his last-hits — the true-damage return is most of your early poke.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W joins the pattern: Q-W on anyone who eats the orb return is a real chunk already.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Charm online — the lane's first kill threat. Hold it for his step-up, not for prayer-range poke.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your charm-orb pick threat out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint your charm-orb pick threat beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Malignance spike: R cooldown drops and every dash window chains a full charm combo.

phases.label: 2+ items

phases.why: Q both ways through the wave on his last-hits — the true-damage return is most of your early poke.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W joins the pattern: Q-W on anyone who eats the orb return is a real chunk already.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Charm online — the lane's first kill threat. Hold it for his step-up, not for prayer-range poke.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — orb every wave contact and bank the passive heals off full clears.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — charm-orb pick threat edge out the windwall dash-weaving when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Yasuo's windwall dash-weaving edge out your charm-orb pick threat on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Ahri is a pick machine: flank dash, charm the carry, and orb the body your team converges on.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Spirit Rush online — three dashes change every rule: charm angles multiply and ganks stop working on you.
